questionSelectionRules.name: Core - Basic Identity & Age Verification
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include basic identity and age questions as foundation for IR-5 parent visa eligibility

actions.reason: Basic identity and age verification confirms parent eligibility for IR-5 visa category, which strictly requires parent to be 21+ years old and child to be USC parent

questionSelectionRules.name: Core - Sponsoring Child Information
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include questions about the sponsoring child's citizenship, employment, and status

actions.reason: Sponsoring child verification is critical for confirming U.S. citizenship status, legal capacity to file I-130 petition, and demonstrated financial stability to support aging parent

questionSelectionRules.name: Core - Parent-Child Relationship Strength
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include relationship verification and communication questions for family bond assessment

actions.reason: Genuine parent-child relationship is cornerstone of IR-5 visa category; ongoing communication and contact history demonstrates family bond authenticity beyond legal obligation

questionSelectionRules.name: Core - Financial Support & Dependency Structure
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include questions about financial support arrangements and dependency

actions.reason: Financial dependency assessment is essential under IR-5; child must demonstrate ability to support parent via I-864 Affidavit of Support; existing support patterns strengthen visa application

questionSelectionRules.name: Core - Post-Arrival Living Plan
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include questions about planned residence and household arrangements in USA

actions.reason: Living arrangements confirm practical integration plan, household stability, proximity to sponsoring child for caregiving, and realistic post-arrival support structure

questionSelectionRules.name: Core - Immigration Intent & Future Plans
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include questions about reasons for immigration and post-arrival plans

actions.reason: Immigration intent assessment confirms genuine desire for family reunification and refutes suspicion of fraudulent petition; critical for age-reduced immigrant requiring extensive vetting

questionSelectionRules.name: Background Security - Travel & Immigration History
questionSelectionRules.description: Always include travel history, visa refusal, and immigration compliance questions

actions.reason: Security and background verification is mandatory for all IR-5 applicants; history of visa refusals, overstays, or criminal activity can result in ineligibility despite family sponsorship

questionSelectionRules.name: Special Case - Widowed Parent
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent is widowed or has lost spouse

actions.reason: Widowed parents often lack spousal income support; requires detailed financial assessment, potential isolation risks, and confirmation of child's capacity to provide emotional and financial support during vulnerable life stage

questionSelectionRules.name: Special Case - Divorced Parent
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent is divorced

actions.reason: Divorced parent status may indicate financial independence or dependency; clarifies whether alimony/support obligations exist and confirms child's actual financial capacity after any spousal obligations

questionSelectionRules.name: Core Financial - Parent Currently Dependent on Child
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent already receives financial support from sponsoring child

actions.reason: Existing financial dependency demonstrates child's proven capacity to support parent and established pattern of filial responsibility; strengthens I-864 Affidavit of Support credibility

questionSelectionRules.name: Financial - Financially Independent Parent
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent has independent income and is not receiving child support

actions.reason: Independent parent with own income reduces public charge concerns; however, must verify income will continue post-immigration and confirm child's I-864 responsibility if parent's resources prove insufficient

questionSelectionRules.name: Financial - Parent Has Current Employment
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent is currently employed

actions.reason: Current employment indicates financial stability; assess transferability of skills to US market and expectation of continued employment post-immigration to reduce public benefits dependency

questionSelectionRules.name: Financial - Retired Parent with Pension or Benefits
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent is retired or relies on pension/social security

actions.reason: Retired parent income assessment critical for determining sufficiency under poverty guidelines; verify pension/social security is stable, portable to USA, and child's supplemental support obligation is secondary

questionSelectionRules.name: Living Arrangements - Will Live with Sponsoring Child
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent plans to live with sponsoring child

actions.reason: Co-residence strengthens family bond verification, confirms caregiving arrangement, and demonstrates immediate post-arrival support structure; addresses common concerns about parent abandonment or insufficient care

questionSelectionRules.name: Living Arrangements - Independent Residence
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent plans to live separately from sponsoring child

actions.reason: Independent living requires detailed explanation of support arrangement, visiting schedule, emergency contact procedures, and how child will provide necessary support and oversight for aging parent

questionSelectionRules.name: Relationship - Strong Parent-Child Communication
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent and child communicate frequently (daily or weekly)

actions.reason: Frequent communication demonstrates active ongoing relationship engagement, emotional closeness, and genuine family bond despite physical separation

questionSelectionRules.name: Relationship - Weak Parent-Child Communication
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent and child communicate rarely (monthly or less)

actions.reason: Infrequent communication raises significant concerns about relationship authenticity and caregiving motivation; requires detailed explanation of communication barriers and demonstrated commitment to family reunification

questionSelectionRules.name: Relationship - Recent Child Visit to Parent
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when sponsoring child has visited parent recently

actions.reason: Recent visit demonstrates active parent-child engagement, financial commitment to travel, and opportunity for in-person relationship strengthening; supports family bond authenticity

questionSelectionRules.name: Relationship - No Recent Child Visit
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when sponsoring child has not visited parent recently

actions.reason: Absence of recent visits requires explanation of barriers (financial, work constraints) and confirmation of alternative relationship maintenance methods; distance does not negate genuine family connection

questionSelectionRules.name: Travel History - Previous US Visit
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent has visited United States before

actions.reason: Previous US visit confirms immigration compliance history, familiarity with US standards of living, opportunity for parent-child bonding, and demonstrates ability to navigate US border procedures

questionSelectionRules.name: Security Alert - Previous Visa Denial
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent has been denied a US visa previously

actions.reason: Previous visa refusal requires detailed explanation of refusal grounds, what circumstances have changed, documentary evidence supporting revised eligibility, and demonstrated commitment to compliance with US immigration law

questionSelectionRules.name: Security Alert - Criminal History
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent has criminal record or legal issues

actions.reason: Criminal history is potential IR-5 visa bar; requires full disclosure, detailed explanation of circumstances, evidence of rehabilitation, and legal disposition; certain crimes result in ineligibility for family-based immigration

questionSelectionRules.name: Age Eligibility - Elderly Parent (65+)
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent is 65 years or older

actions.reason: Elderly parents typically have reduced earning capacity and increased healthcare costs; requires comprehensive financial analysis, verification of sponsoring child's income sufficiency, health status assessment, and determined caregiving plan

questionSelectionRules.name: Age Profile - Parent Under 65
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent is younger (under 65 years old)

actions.reason: Younger parents may have continued employment potential or independent income; focus on financial independence assessment and verification that immigration intent is family reunification rather than economic exploitation

questionSelectionRules.name: Petitioner Family - Child is Married
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when sponsoring child is married

actions.reason: Married sponsoring child's household includes spouse income on I-864 Affidavit; clarifies whether spouse will co-sign support obligation and confirms household stability and size for parent integration

questionSelectionRules.name: Living Arrangements - Household Size Assessment
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when petitioner's household exceeds 3+ members

actions.reason: Large household may indicate resource constraints or elder care experience; assess physical space availability, caregiving capacity, and whether other household members support parent immigration or create conflict

questionSelectionRules.name: Travel History - Extended Stays Outside Home Country
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent has spent 6+ months in countries other than home country

actions.reason: Extended international stays require documentation and explanation; assess whether stays were legal, purpose, and whether pattern indicates intent to remain in US permanently

questionSelectionRules.name: Intent - Clear Permanent Residence Intent
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent expresses clear intent to stay permanently in USA

actions.reason: Clear permanent intent demonstrates genuine immigration purpose aligned with IR-5 visa category intent; eliminates concerns about temporary visa overstay risk or abandonment

questionSelectionRules.name: Intent Alert - Unclear or Conditional Intent
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent expresses uncertain or conditional immigration intent

actions.reason: Uncertain permanent intent raises red flags about IV visa misuse; requires detailed explanation of intent to maintain home country residence, return plans, and how this aligns with family reunification visa category purpose

questionSelectionRules.name: High-Risk Scenario - Visa Denial + Criminal History
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent has BOTH previous visa denial AND criminal record

actions.reason: Combination of visa refusal and criminal history creates significant eligibility concerns; comprehensive security review required with documentary evidence of rehabilitation, changed circumstances, and sponsoring child's understanding of parent's immigration barriers

questionSelectionRules.name: High-Risk Scenario - Weak Communication + Financial Dependency
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent has WEAK communication AND is financially dependent on child

actions.reason: Combination of minimal communication and existing dependency raises concerns about whether petitioner views parent as obligation rather than family; requires detailed demonstration of genuine family care motivation beyond financial support mechanism

questionSelectionRules.name: Strong Family Bond - Frequent Communication + Recent Visit
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent has STRONG communication AND child has visited recently

actions.reason: Strong communication combined with recent visits demonstrates active, engaged family relationship; significantly strengthens visa application credibility and family reunification intent verification

questionSelectionRules.name: Favorable Profile - Independent Parent with Stable Income
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent has own income/employment AND is not financially dependent

actions.reason: Financially independent parent reduces public charge concerns and I-864 burden on sponsoring child; demonstrates self-sufficiency and reduces likelihood of government benefit dependency post-immigration

questionSelectionRules.name: Special Circumstance - Elderly Dependent Parent 75+
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent is 75 years or older, indicating advanced age and likely dependency

actions.reason: Advanced age (75+) indicates high likelihood of significant caregiving needs, healthcare expenses, and potential long-term care requirements; requires comprehensive financial and caregiving plan verification, health insurance assessment, and family support commitment

questionSelectionRules.name: Special Case - Parent's Marital Status Impact
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied based on parent's current marital status for dependency and support assessment

actions.reason: Unmarried parent (widow, divorced, single) typically has sole dependency on sponsoring child; marital status affects financial assessment, available resources, and support structure verification

questionSelectionRules.name: Relationship Strength - Multiple Communication Channels
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent and child use diverse communication methods (phone, video, chat, etc.)

actions.reason: Multiple communication methods demonstrate accessibility, technological capability, diverse relationship engagement, and adaptability to maintain connection

questionSelectionRules.name: Intent Quality - Clear and Detailed Post-Arrival Plans
questionSelectionRules.description: Applied when parent provides thoughtful, specific plans for life in USA

actions.reason: Clear articulation of realistic post-arrival plans demonstrates thoughtfulness, genuine intent assessment, and reduces concerns about lack of planning or parental abandonment expectations

questionSelectionRules.name: Final Eligibility - Comprehensive Assessment Required
questionSelectionRules.description: Meta-rule for final consolidated assessment of all major factors

actions.reason: IR-5 parent visa requires comprehensive evaluation of identity, family relationship genuineness, financial capacity, and security background; no single factor is dispositive, but combination creates overall eligibility assessment
